title: Daily Briefing — May 22
headline: Tech leads, but rotation is brewing: flows hint at sector catch-up while greed fades.
body: Right, big picture: The S&P (SPY $742.72) remains in rally mode, closing well above its 20 MA at $730—that’s your mean reversion zone, and price is respecting it. VIX is chill at 16.8, trending below its own 20 MA, so volatility is contracting. Fear & Greed sits at 58 (plain greed, but not extreme), pulling back from last month’s 68.5—so retail is cooling off, but still leaning positive.

Sector rotation? XLK (Tech) is firmly leading (RS 117.5, Mom 101.0), with big names like NVDA ($219.51) and MSFT ($419.09) cruising above 20 MAs. But watch healthcare (XLV, RS 87.8, Mom 103.3) and staples (XLP), both improving from laggard to catch-up territory. Financials (XLF $51.73) are approaching breakout above all major MAs—a canary looking healthy for now, but keep an eye if that 200 MA at $52.27 acts as resistance.

Contrarian signal: We’re not seeing a put/call or AAII extreme; Smart Money isn't screaming panic, and there’s no record retail pile-in. Still, be suspicious of monster moves like ARM (+16.2%) and NBIS (+14.7%)—don’t chase, abundance mindset. Materials (XLB) risk remains elevated even as momentum improves, so if the press starts parroting 'commodities comeback,' remember: if it’s in the press, it’s in the price.
callout_body:
SPY: $730 (20 MA), $694.87 (50 MA) — mean reversion triggers
NVDA: $214.39 (20 MA), $196.16 (50 MA) — support zones
XLK: $169.55 (20 MA), $143.79 (200 MA) — lead sector
XLF: $52.27 (200 MA) — resistance canary
VIX: 16.8 (<17.63 20 MA) — low volatility regime
Fear & Greed: 58 — not extreme
ARM: $221.06 (20 MA) — don't chase breakout
action_items: Stay long core tech names above their 20 MAs; trail stops just below for risk management.
Start watching for sector rotation plays in healthcare (XLV) and staples (XLP) as momentum improves—wait for confirmation above 20/50 MAs.
closing_quote: If you missed ARM or NBIS, breathe. Abundance mindset—next trade, not last trade.
